title: Contact-Net Outbreak Clustering: One Algorithm, Many Transmission Geometries
strategies: trivial: put every individual in one outbreak cluster -- structurally valid so it earns the 0.1 floor, but ARI=0 on every dataset, geometric mean pinned at 0.1

greedy: Lloyd k-means on the raw exposure coordinates with deterministic k-means++ seeding; recovers round household blobs but its Euclidean/spherical bias collapses on ring, chain and concentric transmission geometries

strong: standardize, build a symmetric kNN affinity graph, take the normalized-Laplacian spectral embedding, and k-means the row-normalized eigenvectors with deterministic restarts -- the connectivity view transfers across blobs, moons, rings and elongated chains
invalid: emit a wrong-length labeling containing NaN; strict validation rejects it -> 0 on every dataset -> geometric mean 0
